# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

---
name: gcp-app
description: Deployment Manager template to deploy a GCP application. Uses the Google Compute Engine, Google SQL, Google Cloud Storage and Google Load Balancer resources.  Excludes Cloud Logging, Monitoring configuration (since this is our initial MVP deployment).
resources:
  - name: app-instance
    type: compute.v1.instance
    properties:
      machineType: zonal/n2-standard-8
      zone: us-central1-f

      # Network configuration, setting the network, subnetwork and default IP
      # ranges to 'default' ensures that GCP's internal services operate in
      # a private manner; in production environments we should be using one of 
      # GCP's reserved ranges. 
      networkInterfaces:
        - accessConfigs:
            - name: external-nat
              type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
          network: projects/${project}/global/networks/default

      metadata:

        # Disable the GCP instance metadata service, setting the source image to
        # 'ubuntu-1804-lts' ensures that GCP's internal services operate in a private 
        # manner; in production environments we should be using one of GCP's reserved ranges.
      - name: disable-metadata
        value:  "True"

      serviceAccounts:

        # Disable the GCP instance metadata service, setting the source image to 'ubuntu-1804-lts' ensures that GCP's internal services operate in a private manner; in production environments we should be using one of GCP's reserved ranges.
      - email: <EMAIL>
        scopes:
          - cloud-platform

      scheduling:
        automaticRestart: true
        onHostMaintenance: MIGRATE
        preemptible: true
    metadata:
      # Set labels to ensure that instances created from this template are properly filtered by the appropriate environments.
      labels: 
        - env = "prod"
    # Cloud SQL instance for production, and a private Google Compute Engine network and firewall rule to allow access.
    networksInterfaces:
        - name : public-network
          accessConfigs:
              - name: external
                type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
          network: projects/${project}/global/networks/default
        - name: private-network
      rules:
        - protocol: all
          action: ALLOW

    # Create a Cloud Storage bucket and attach a firewall rule to it, so that the GCP instances have access.
    disks:
      - deviceName: default-disk1
        mode: READ_WRITE
        boot: true
